title: The Myth of Originality
author: From: Steal Like an Artist
content: Nothing is entirely original. Innovation is the act of remixing existing ideas into a new context.
action: Remix two unrelated concepts into one new idea.

title: Deliberate Practice
author: From: Peak
content: Practice is not just repetition. It is focused, goal-oriented activity designed to improve a specific weakness.
action: Spend 20 minutes specifically targeting a weakness.

title: The 2-Minute Rule
author: From: Getting Things Done
content: If a task takes less than two minutes, do it immediately. Don't archive it, don't defer it.
action: Execute all pending tasks that take under two minutes.

title: Strategic Inaction
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: Sometimes the most strategic move is to do absolutely nothing. Not every problem requires an intervention.
action: Identify and pause one active, low-value project.

title: The Power of Defaults
author: From: Predictably Irrational
content: People stick to the path of least resistance. Make the 'right' choice the default choice in your environment.
action: Adjust your digital settings to enforce a productive default.
